description: Instructions / tools available to the LLM compiler. Each tool defines an operation on a property row (referred to as 'this'). Column values are accessed as 'this.column_name'. Produce an 'expression' that evaluates to boolean or numeric for each 'clear' constraint.

tools.name: compare_numeric
tools.description: Compare a numeric column to a constant. The column is accessed as this.<col>.

tools.name: compare_string
tools.syntax: this.<col> == '<value>'  |  this.<col> in [...]  |  this.<col> not in [...]
tools.description: Compare a string/keyword column to a constant or set. City names must be standard English. Canton codes must be 2-letter uppercase.

tools.name: boolean_check
tools.description: Check whether a boolean property column is present (true) or absent (false).

tools.name: set_membership
tools.syntax: this.<col> in [v1, v2, ...]  |  this.<col> not in [v1, v2, ...]
tools.description: Check if the column value is in (or not in) a set of constants.

tools.name: date_compare
tools.description: Compare a date column to a fixed ISO date. Use for 'available from' style constraints on non-predefined keys.

tools.name: distance
tools.description: Computes straight-line distance in kilometres between the property coordinates and a fixed target location. The LLM must resolve the target's latitude and longitude from its knowledge and inline exact float values. Use for user requests like 'near ETH', 'close to EPFL', 'within 3 km of Zurich HB', etc.

tools.name: distance_column
tools.description: For predefined distance columns (distance_public_transport, distance_shop, distance_kindergarten, distance_school_1, distance_school_2): compare to a threshold in metres. Prefer this over the distance() function when a matching column exists.

notes: All expressions operate on a single property row referred to as 'this'. Access column values via this.column_name.
The result of evaluating a clear expression against a row is boolean (true/false).
For distance to named places (ETH, EPFL, landmarks): use the distance() tool with inlined lat/lon from the landmark_examples table above or from your knowledge.
For distance to generic amenities with a predefined column (shop, school, transport): use distance_column with this.distance_* and a metre threshold.
Constraints with no resolvable column+expression (subjective vibes, unknown landmarks) must be tagged clarity='vague' and must NOT have an 'expression' field.
